title: cerastium alpinum f. lanatum
intro: Cerastium alpinum f. lanatum, also known as woolly mountain chickweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, and it has been used to treat various ailments such as skin irritations, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Cerastium alpinum f. lanatum can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or ointment, making it a versatile and accessible medicinal plant.
